title: Pocket Fleet Shell
concept: A mobile-first compact WordPress Playground shell for power users managing many saved sites, with bottom navigation, stacked touch panels, and a desktop split preview/editor workspace.
focus: saved Playground fleet management as the first viewport
distinct launch and import route contracts
explicit browser storage versus local directory save destinations
high-contrast black, white, and cyan technical palette
flows: select a launch source from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or .zip import
save a temporary Playground to browser storage or to a local directory with progress and resulting saved state
manage saved Playgrounds with rename, delete, saved identity, local-directory identity, and unsaved temporary state
navigate the active WordPress path, refresh the preview, and jump to WP Admin or Homepage
use Site Manager settings, files, Blueprint, database, and logs from touch-friendly tabs
browse, filter, search, inspect, and run Blueprint gallery entries from a dense catalog
export to GitHub, download a .zip, run a Blueprint URL, import a .zip over the current site, and see replacement consequences
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
edit WordPress, PHP, and language settings
understand destructive reset and reload behavior
use File browser with create folder, create file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
